A `surprise` marks where your world model is wrong -- re-probe there instead of pressing on.

For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check where the agent is now and whether it crossed the purple bar.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
print("After LEFT x2:")
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"  boundary={n['boundary']} center_color={next(x['color'] for x in seg if x['id'] in n['children'])}")

# Check area around purple bar
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nAround purple bar (rows 27-32, cols 33-45):")
for r in range(27, 33):
    if r < len(ascii_lines):
        print(f"{r:3d}: {ascii_lines[r][33:45]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The agent is at cols 39-41 now. The purple bar is at cols 33-38. So the agent is at the edge of the purple bar but hasn't crossed it. Let me try one more LEFT to see if it can cross.
Actually, looking at the ascii, cols 33-38 are pppppp (purple), and cols 39-41 are NNN (agent). So the agent is right next to the purple bar. Let me try LEFT again to see if it can cross or if it's blocked.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Agent at cols 39-41, purple bar at cols 33-38
# Try one more LEFT to see if blocked by purple bar

predict(expect_change=True, note='Test if agent blocked by purple bar')
action(['LEFT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
